ZEAL/BZea Prolificacy (Prolif) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER

  • Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • View Source

The prolificacy analog of the EN notebook: same SNP50K ancestry pipeline, phenotype = per-field SpATS prolificacy BLUEs, candidate set = the curated maize ear-number / prolificacy cloned-gene panel (core gt1/tb1/tru1 + inflorescence-architecture branching/meristem/KRN genes). Significance is a genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (per scan): the Manhattan line is the −log₁₀P exceeded by only 5% of permutations, and candidate/peak calls report whether they clear it.

Author

Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata

Published

July 14, 2026

What this is. The prolificacy (Prolif) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-en-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real BZea pipeline end-to-end: field Prolif → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. Prolif is prolificacy — the propensity to bear more than one ear — and the per-genotype value is the per-field SpATS mean across the two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5). It shares the ear-number candidate panel with the ear-number (EN) notebook; only the phenotype changes.

ImportantGenotype at 0.4× skim: why ancestry, not per-SNP

SNP50K is 51,991 teosinte-vs-B73 sites pileup-genotyped at ~0.4× — hard genotypes are unusable (70% missing, ~0% hom-ALT), so ancestry is inferred from the per-sample allele counts. The per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (Panel B) is therefore λ_GC-deflated (sparse mean-imputation); the RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C) is the usable, block-smoothed genotype (teosinte presence 0.111, matching the established BzeaSeq ~0.101). This is the SNP50K-specific reason ancestry inference is mandatory, and is identical to the DTA/PH/EH/EN notebooks — only the phenotype changes.

Trait context. Prolificacy — bearing multiple ears rather than a single dominant ear — is the domestication axis most directly opposed between teosinte and B73: teosinte’s long, branched lateral architecture produces many ears, while the maize apical-dominance program concentrates resources into one or two. Prolif and ear number (EN) are two scorings of the same underlying axillary-meristem-fate biology and share the candidate panel: the core regulators gt1 (grassy tillers1, the prol1.1 QTL gene — teosinte allele derepresses axillary ear buds → prolific ears), tb1 (teosinte branched1, the Hopscotch-driven apical-dominance domestication locus), and tru1 (tassels replace upper ears1, a tb1 target for ear-vs-tassel identity); plus the second-tier inflorescence-architecture / meristem / KRN genes (ub2/ub3, ra1/ra2/ra3, fea2/fea3/td1). Because teosinte ancestry is broadly prolificacy-increasing and partly collinear with K, the mixed model absorbs ancestry-correlated variance, and the model-independent JLM (nested-in-family stepwise) is the primary QTL tool — with gt1/tb1 the two loci most likely to surface as headline, effect-bearing QTL.

Show code 
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code 
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "PROLIF"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)
NoteSignificance = 1000-permutation genome-wide FWER (5%)

Every Manhattan’s dotted line is the −log₁₀P exceeded by only 5% of 1000 permutations of that scan (a genome-wide max-−log₁₀P null; scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R), stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv. A marker is significant iff its −log₁₀P clears that line. The line’s height differs per panel with the null. Candidate/peak tables report whether the marker clears the 5% FWER line.

Candidate gene registry

Curated evidence for each candidate (tier, causal direction, evidence type, phenotype, and a DOI link) from the tracked registry (reference/candidate_genes.csv × candidate_evidence.csv). These are the genes starred on the Manhattans and labelled on the lollipop; loci without B73 v5 coordinates still appear here but are not plotted.

Show code 
candidate_table(TR)
Candidate genes for PROLIF from the tracked registry (reference/candidate_*.csv): 12 loci (12 mapped to B73 v5, 0 unmapped). DOI links where curated; “TODO” = evidence not yet curated.
gene full name locus tier direction evidence phenotype / note reference
gt1 grassy tillers1 1:23.4 Mb 1 - ear number / prolificacy (core) Ear number / prolificacy — underlies the prol1.1 QTL; represses growth of axillary ear buds. Repressed by shade; teosinte allele → prolific tillers/ears. 10.1073/pnas.1102819108
ub2 unbranched2 1:192.4 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Inflorescence branching / kernel row number — tassel branch & ear row number. 10.1073/pnas.1407401112
tb1 teosinte branched1 1:272.3 Mb 1 - ear number / prolificacy (core) Ear number / prolificacy — suppresses axillary branching; Hopscotch insertion upstream drives apical dominance & reduced ear number. Major domestication locus. 10.1038/386485a0
abi17 ABI3-VP1-transcription factor 17 2:49.4 Mb 3 - chr2 positional candidate Ear number / prolificacy — chr2 positional candidate (abi17, MaizeGDB). Browser/MaizeMine ‘Auxin response factor’ auto-annotation is a B3-domain artifact. MaizeGDB (Zm00001eb082150 = abi17); positional candidate under chr2 QTL
ra2 ramosa2 3:12.8 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Inflorescence branching — meristem determinacy in ear/tassel. 10.1105/tpc.105.039032
fea3 fasciated ear3 3:29.2 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Ear meristem size / kernel row number — CLE-peptide signalling. 10.1038/ng.3567
tru1 tassels replace upper ears1 3:152.1 Mb 1 - ear number / prolificacy (core) Ear number / prolificacy — direct target of tb1; promotes ear (vs tassel) identity in axillary meristems. Loss → tassels replace upper ears. 10.1073/pnas.1714960114
fea2 fasciated ear2 4:138.7 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Ear meristem size / kernel row & ear fasciation — CLV-WUS pathway. 10.1101/gad.208501
ub3 unbranched3 4:205.4 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Inflorescence branching / kernel row number — paralog of ub2. 10.1073/pnas.1407401112
td1 thick tassel dwarf1 5:63.5 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Ear/tassel meristem size — CLV-WUS pathway; kernel row number. 10.1242/dev.01671
ra1 ramosa1 7:115.0 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Inflorescence branching — restricts long-branch (spikelet-pair) formation; affects ear/tassel branch number. 10.1038/nature03892
ra3 ramosa3 7:176.0 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Inflorescence branching — meristem determinacy; sugar signalling. 10.1038/nature04725

Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker

For each maize ear-number / prolificacy candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Family+K) scan (Panel C). Candidates are the curated cloned ear-number / prolificacy panel (core gt1/tb1/tru1 + inflorescence-architecture branching/meristem/KRN genes; v5 coordinates); pathway records the tier and protein/role each acts in.

Show code 
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/prolif_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("PROLIF(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("Prolif candidates (B73 v5; curated maize ear-number / prolificacy panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Family+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)
Prolif candidates (B73 v5; curated maize ear-number / prolificacy panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Family+K) scan; ‘clears perm 5%’ = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = 4.53).
gene pathway chr gene start (v5) closest Δstart (bp) closest -log10P window-max -log10P clears perm 5%
gt1 Ear number / prolificacy — underlies the prol1.1 QTL; represses growth of axillary ear buds. Repressed by shade; teosinte allele → prolific tillers/ears. 1 23,433,554 -1,058 2.99 4.48 FALSE
ub2 Inflorescence branching / kernel row number — tassel branch & ear row number. 1 192,381,440 -101,208 0.85 1.09 FALSE
tb1 Ear number / prolificacy — suppresses axillary branching; Hopscotch insertion upstream drives apical dominance & reduced ear number. Major domestication locus. 1 272,330,564 -40,634 0.04 0.30 FALSE
abi17 Ear number / prolificacy — chr2 positional candidate (abi17, MaizeGDB). Browser/MaizeMine ‘Auxin response factor’ auto-annotation is a B3-domain artifact. 2 49,388,328 2,821 0.33 0.41 FALSE
ra2 Inflorescence branching — meristem determinacy in ear/tassel. 3 12,830,057 7,984 1.53 1.92 FALSE
fea3 Ear meristem size / kernel row number — CLE-peptide signalling. 3 29,213,107 -40,521 0.78 1.43 FALSE
tru1 Ear number / prolificacy — direct target of tb1; promotes ear (vs tassel) identity in axillary meristems. Loss → tassels replace upper ears. 3 152,086,191 -7,157 0.41 1.00 FALSE
fea2 Ear meristem size / kernel row & ear fasciation — CLV-WUS pathway. 4 138,680,814 -5,446 1.04 1.04 FALSE
ub3 Inflorescence branching / kernel row number — paralog of ub2. 4 205,414,194 13,112 0.75 0.75 FALSE
td1 Ear/tassel meristem size — CLV-WUS pathway; kernel row number. 5 63,488,542 -1,077 0.77 0.77 FALSE
ra1 Inflorescence branching — restricts long-branch (spikelet-pair) formation; affects ear/tassel branch number. 7 114,958,643 29,660 0.44 0.95 FALSE
ra3 Inflorescence branching — meristem determinacy; sugar signalling. 7 175,982,697 3,277 0.71 1.45 FALSE

OLS → MLM: structure control

Here Family is the 82 donor-accession fixed factor (parent-plant-level structure correction) that the MLM and per-marker OLS scans were actually computed with — a stronger correction than the 5 donor taxa the JLM nests on. It likely over-corrects population structure and costs some power; a taxon-level (5) MLM/OLS rerun is a pending comparison (see TERMINOLOGY.md).

The fixed-effect OLS (Family + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Family + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor); K deflates λ toward 1 and removes the ancestry-correlated bulk that a near-isogenic, teosinte-ancestry-structured prolificacy panel carries.

Show code 
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea Prolif — OLS (Family + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)

The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (ancestry-block LD) — the same structure artifact the MLM K term removes below.

Show code 
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Family + marker, no K)", "MLM (Family + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Family fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan's 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.")
OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Family fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan’s 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.
model markers lambda_GC n > perm 5% max_logP
OLS (Family + marker, no K) 48900 11.61 5955 13.35
MLM (Family + K) 48900 1.09 9 5.14
Show code 
ov <- fread(overlap)
peak_at <- function(scan, ch, s) {
  w <- scan[CHR == ch & abs(BP - s) <= 5e5 & is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  if (!nrow(w)) NA_real_ else round(max(-log10(w$P)), 2)
}
cmp <- ov[, .(
  gene = symbol, chr,
  `OLS peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(ols), chr, start),
  `MLM peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(mlm), chr, start)
)]
cmp[, `drop (OLS−MLM)` := round(`OLS peak` - `MLM peak`, 2)]
setorder(cmp, -`OLS peak`)
knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM. K suppresses the ancestry-collinear prolificacy signal.")
Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM. K suppresses the ancestry-collinear prolificacy signal.
gene chr OLS peak MLM peak drop (OLS−MLM)
gt1 1 11.86 4.48 7.38
ra2 3 10.71 1.92 8.79
ub3 4 8.86 0.75 8.11
fea2 4 7.47 1.04 6.43
fea3 3 7.16 1.43 5.73
ra3 7 4.65 1.45 3.20
tru1 3 3.94 1.00 2.94
abi17 2 3.04 0.41 2.63
ra1 7 2.53 0.95 1.58
td1 5 1.54 0.77 0.77
ub2 1 1.21 1.09 0.12
tb1 1 0.74 0.30 0.44

JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)

Prolif QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, entry by a 1000-permutation empirical α = 0.05 (Chen-style genome-wide FWER; per-trait)). JLM QTL are selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan permutation FWER line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — the primary Prolif analysis, as in Chen 2019.

Show code 
p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/prolif_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL/BZea Prolif — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli

MLM (Family + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C)

Show code 
p_mlm <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea Prolif — MLM (Family + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm, out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
p_mlm
Show code 
cat(sprintf("%d markers clear the 5%% FWER line (-log10P = %.2f)\n", nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm), thr_mlm))
9 markers clear the 5% FWER line (-log10P = 4.53)

Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)

Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4× is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the demonstration that ancestry inference is mandatory at low coverage — identical in mechanism to the DTA/PH/EH/EN notebooks.

Show code 
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("Prolif — MLM (Family+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("Prolif — MLM (Family+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

Multi-caller Prolif scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)

The same Prolif MLM (Family+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (Prolif) changes.

Show code 
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/prolif_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
# best (max -log10P) Prolif candidate window per caller
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "Prolif MLM (Family+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the ear-number / prolificacy candidate windows.")
Prolif MLM (Family+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller’s own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the ear-number / prolificacy candidate windows.
caller teosinte presence lambda_GC n > perm 5% best candidate -log10P
control 0.008 0.78 17 5.17
nNIL 0.018 0.83 3 6.67
RTIGER 0.111 1.09 9 4.48
LB-Impute 0.035 0.90 154 2.56
binhmm 0.101 0.86 0 3.27
Show code 
panels <- lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", callers[[nm]])))
  plot_manhattan(sc, sprintf("Prolif MLM (Family+K) — %s (λ_GC = %.2f; perm FWER 5%%)", nm, lam(sc$P)), overlap_csv = overlap, lod = gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", callers[[nm]]))
})
cc <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = panels, ncol = 1, labels = LETTERS[1:5], label_size = 18, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_caller_composite_fdr_snp50k.png"), cc, width = 9, height = 20, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
cc
Source Code
---
title: "ZEAL/BZea Prolificacy (Prolif) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER"
subtitle: "The prolificacy analog of the EN notebook: same SNP50K ancestry pipeline, phenotype = per-field SpATS prolificacy BLUEs, candidate set = the curated maize ear-number / prolificacy cloned-gene panel (core gt1/tb1/tru1 + inflorescence-architecture branching/meristem/KRN genes). Significance is a genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (per scan): the Manhattan line is the −log₁₀P exceeded by only 5% of permutations, and candidate/peak calls report whether they clear it."
author: "Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---

**What this is.** The prolificacy (Prolif) sibling of
[`zeal-qtl-recovery-en-mlm-snp50k`](zeal-qtl-recovery-en-mlm-snp50k.html), built from the
same real BZea pipeline end-to-end: field Prolif → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry
dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic
B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from **5 teosinte taxa**. Prolif is **prolificacy** — the propensity to bear
more than one ear — and the per-genotype value is the per-field SpATS mean across the two fields
(CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5). It shares the ear-number candidate panel with the
[`ear-number (EN)`](zeal-qtl-recovery-en-mlm-snp50k.html) notebook; only the phenotype changes.

::: {.callout-important}
## Genotype at 0.4× skim: why ancestry, not per-SNP
SNP50K is 51,991 teosinte-vs-B73 sites pileup-genotyped at ~0.4× — hard genotypes are
unusable (70% missing, ~0% hom-ALT), so ancestry is inferred from the per-sample allele
**counts**. The **per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (Panel B)** is therefore λ_GC-deflated (sparse
mean-imputation); the **RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C)** is the usable, block-smoothed
genotype (teosinte presence 0.111, matching the established BzeaSeq ~0.101). This is the
SNP50K-specific reason ancestry inference is mandatory, and is identical to the DTA/PH/EH/EN
notebooks — only the phenotype changes.
:::

**Trait context.** Prolificacy — bearing multiple ears rather than a single dominant ear — is the
domestication axis most directly opposed between teosinte and B73: teosinte's long, branched lateral
architecture produces many ears, while the maize apical-dominance program concentrates resources
into one or two. Prolif and [ear number (EN)](zeal-qtl-recovery-en-mlm-snp50k.html) are two
scorings of the same underlying axillary-meristem-fate biology and share the candidate panel: the
**core regulators** `gt1` (*grassy tillers1*, the **prol1.1** QTL gene — teosinte allele derepresses
axillary ear buds → prolific ears), `tb1` (*teosinte branched1*, the *Hopscotch*-driven
apical-dominance domestication locus), and `tru1` (*tassels replace upper ears1*, a tb1 target for
ear-vs-tassel identity); plus the second-tier **inflorescence-architecture / meristem / KRN** genes
(`ub2`/`ub3`, `ra1`/`ra2`/`ra3`, `fea2`/`fea3`/`td1`). Because teosinte ancestry is broadly
prolificacy-increasing and **partly collinear with K**, the mixed model absorbs ancestry-correlated
variance, and the model-independent **JLM** (nested-in-family stepwise) is the primary QTL tool —
with `gt1`/`tb1` the two loci most likely to surface as headline, effect-bearing QTL.

```{r setup}
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
```

```{r load}
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "PROLIF"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Significance = 1000-permutation genome-wide FWER (5%)
Every Manhattan's dotted line is the **−log₁₀P exceeded by only 5% of 1000 permutations** of
that scan (a genome-wide max-−log₁₀P null; `scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R`), stored per
(trait, model, geno) in `data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv`. A marker is significant iff its
−log₁₀P clears that line. The line's height differs per panel with the null. Candidate/peak
tables report whether the marker clears the 5% FWER line.
:::

## Candidate gene registry

Curated evidence for each **candidate** (tier, causal direction, evidence type, phenotype, and a DOI link) from the tracked registry (`reference/candidate_genes.csv` × `candidate_evidence.csv`). These are the genes starred on the Manhattans and labelled on the lollipop; loci without B73 v5 coordinates still appear here but are not plotted.

```{r candidate-registry}
candidate_table(TR)
```

## Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker

For each maize ear-number / prolificacy candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs
the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic **MLM (Family+K)** scan (Panel C).
Candidates are the curated cloned ear-number / prolificacy panel (core `gt1`/`tb1`/`tru1` +
inflorescence-architecture branching/meristem/KRN genes; v5 coordinates); `pathway` records the
tier and protein/role each acts in.

```{r candidate-markers}
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/prolif_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("PROLIF(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("Prolif candidates (B73 v5; curated maize ear-number / prolificacy panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Family+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)
```

## OLS → MLM: structure control

Here **Family** is the **82 donor-accession** fixed factor (parent-plant-level structure correction) that the MLM and per-marker OLS scans were actually computed with — a stronger correction than the 5 donor **taxa** the JLM nests on. It likely over-corrects population structure and costs some power; a taxon-level (5) MLM/OLS rerun is a pending comparison (see `TERMINOLOGY.md`).

The fixed-effect OLS (Family + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Family + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same
fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor);
K deflates λ toward 1 and removes the ancestry-correlated bulk that a near-isogenic,
teosinte-ancestry-structured prolificacy panel carries.

```{r ols-manhattan, fig.width=9, fig.height=4.4}
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea Prolif — OLS (Family + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
```

The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (ancestry-block LD) — the same structure
artifact the MLM K term removes below.

```{r ols-vs-mlm}
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Family + marker, no K)", "MLM (Family + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Family fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan's 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.")
```

```{r ols-vs-mlm-peaks}
ov <- fread(overlap)
peak_at <- function(scan, ch, s) {
  w <- scan[CHR == ch & abs(BP - s) <= 5e5 & is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  if (!nrow(w)) NA_real_ else round(max(-log10(w$P)), 2)
}
cmp <- ov[, .(
  gene = symbol, chr,
  `OLS peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(ols), chr, start),
  `MLM peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(mlm), chr, start)
)]
cmp[, `drop (OLS−MLM)` := round(`OLS peak` - `MLM peak`, 2)]
setorder(cmp, -`OLS peak`)
knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM. K suppresses the ancestry-collinear prolificacy signal.")
```

## JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)

Prolif QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, entry by a 1000-permutation empirical α = 0.05 (Chen-style genome-wide FWER; per-trait)). JLM QTL are
selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan
permutation FWER line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — the primary Prolif analysis, as
in Chen 2019.

```{r lollipop-jlm, fig.width=9, fig.height=4}
p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/prolif_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL/BZea Prolif — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli
```

## MLM (Family + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C)

```{r manhattan-mlm, fig.width=9, fig.height=4.4}
p_mlm <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea Prolif — MLM (Family + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm, out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
p_mlm
cat(sprintf("%d markers clear the 5%% FWER line (-log10P = %.2f)\n", nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm), thr_mlm))
```

## Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)

Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4×
is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry
mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔C contrast is the notebook's
per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the demonstration that ancestry inference is
mandatory at low coverage — identical in mechanism to the DTA/PH/EH/EN notebooks.

```{r composite, fig.width=9, fig.height=13}
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("Prolif — MLM (Family+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("Prolif — MLM (Family+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite
```

## Multi-caller Prolif scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)

The same Prolif MLM (Family+K) scan on each ancestry caller's recovered genotypes, at the real
~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The robust, trait-independent finding is **teosinte-presence
recovery**: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established
BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL,
LB-Impute) **under-call**. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below).
Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (Prolif) changes.

```{r caller-table}
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/prolif_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
# best (max -log10P) Prolif candidate window per caller
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "Prolif MLM (Family+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the ear-number / prolificacy candidate windows.")
```

```{r caller-composite, fig.width=9, fig.height=20}
panels <- lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/prolif_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", callers[[nm]])))
  plot_manhattan(sc, sprintf("Prolif MLM (Family+K) — %s (λ_GC = %.2f; perm FWER 5%%)", nm, lam(sc$P)), overlap_csv = overlap, lod = gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", callers[[nm]]))
})
cc <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = panels, ncol = 1, labels = LETTERS[1:5], label_size = 18, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/prolif_caller_composite_fdr_snp50k.png"), cc, width = 9, height = 20, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
cc
```
